channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania Boosts Health Sector with Korean Investment Project
item.description: The article highlights Tanzania's recent initiatives to strengthen its healthcare infrastructure through a significant investment from South Korea. A 7.26 billion Tanzanian shilling project is set to ...

item.title: Photographer Captures Mesmerizing Lavender Fields in Southern France
item.description: The article highlights the breathtaking lavender fields of Valensole in Southern France, captured through the lens of self-taught photographer Samir Belhamra. These images, taken at night, showcase th...

item.title: DFA Clarifies No China Approval Needed for Ayungin Shoal Missions
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) clarified that the Philippines does not need China’s permission to conduct rotation and resupply missions to Ayungin (Second Thomas) Shoal. The 2024 provisional...

item.title: Trump's Condition on Saudi Nuclear Deal: Normalisation with Israel
item.description: US President Donald Trump has announced that the previously agreed nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia, allowing the Gulf Kingdom to enrich uranium, will now be contingent on Saudi Arabia normalising ties ...

item.title: Anton Jäger's 'Hyperpolitics' Explores the Fragmented Nature of Modern Political Movements
item.description: Anton Jäger's book 'Hyperpolitics: Extreme Politicization without Political Consequences' examines the evolution of political engagement in the 21st century. Jäger outlines four eras: mass politics (1...

item.title: Chongolo sets four-month deadline for Karatu onion market completion
item.description: The news highlights the government's decision to set a four-month deadline for the completion of the Karatu onion market project. This initiative aims to address delays in infrastructure development t...

item.title: House Committee Demands Suspension of NRS Recruitment Amid Constitutional Disputes
item.description: The House of Representatives Committee on Federal Character has condemned the Nigeria Revenue Service (NRS) Chairman, Dr. Zacch Adedeji, for disregarding the National Assembly's constitutional authori...

item.title: Houthi attacks drive oil prices near $100 as supply routes face disruption
item.description: Crude oil prices hit new highs as Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers in the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb Strait spark fears of global supply disruptions. Brent crude approached $100 per barrel, up 4.21% to ...

item.title: The Westies soundtrack features a mix of punk and Irish music
item.description: The Westies, a crime drama series created by Chris Brancato and Michael Panes, explores the tensions within New York's Irish-American gang during the early 1980s. The show, available on MGM+, blends p...

item.title: Wildfire Evacuees from Ontario First Nations Struggle with Mental Health and Resource Challenges
item.description: Over 2,800 wildfire evacuees from First Nations communities in Ontario's far north are facing significant challenges, including mental health struggles, limited access to essential resources, and syst...

item.title: Former Admiral Explains U.S. Strategic Reasons Behind Nuclear Cooperation Deal With Saudi Arabia
item.description: Former U.S. Navy Admiral James Stavridis discussed the strategic motivations behind a proposed nuclear cooperation agreement between the United States and Saudi Arabia during an interview with CNN's A...

item.title: Celebrity Shares Leaked Chat Showing Woman Asks For Marriage After Baby Daddy Left Her
item.description: Kikuyu content creator Karangu Muraya has shared another leaked inbox message from a woman who asked him to marry her, claiming her baby daddy abandoned her. The chat, posted on his Facebook page, sho...

item.title: NBI launches investigation into alleged anomalies in SEA Games funding
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has formed a specialized task force called 'Task Force Cash Cow' to examine potential irregularities in the Philippines' management of the 2019 Southeast Asi...

item.title: Miami Heat Apologize for Mistakenly Posting LeBron James Presser Link
item.description: The Miami Heat issued an apology after mistakenly posting a link to a scheduled live stream for LeBron James' introductory press conference on their YouTube channel. A team spokesperson confirmed the ...

item.title: Free State judge tells tribunal his long absence was due to serious health problems
item.description: Free State High Court judge Lepona Lekale has rejected claims that he committed gross misconduct or was incompetent after spending eight years on paid sick leave while continuing to receive his salary...

item.title: Young Slash's Early Life and Musical Beginnings in 1983
item.description: This article explores the early life of Saul Hudson, known as Slash, when he was 18 years old living in Los Angeles in 1983. At this time, he was deeply involved in the local rock scene, having transi...

item.title: Troubleshooting access issues for News Corp Australia website
item.description: The article explains that users may encounter a blockage as a crawler bot when accessing News Corp Australia websites. The company uses software to manage crawler traffic, and if a user is not a bot b...

item.title: Lamu East MP Backs Dangote's KSh 2.2 Trillion Refinery for Economic Growth and Jobs
item.description: MP Ruweida Mohamed has strongly supported Aliko Dangote's KSh 2.2 trillion oil refinery project in Lamu County, highlighting its potential to boost local employment and economic revival. The project, ...

item.title: Google Faces EU Fines but Seeks to Avoid Further Penalties Through Dialogue
item.description: Google, the tech giant under Alphabet, has been fined a total of €890 million ($1 billion) by the European Commission for violating EU regulations under the Digital Markets Act (DMA). The fines, total...

item.title: South Africa's Education System Adapts to a 50% Population Increase Since 1994
item.description: The article explores how South Africa's education system has managed to adapt to a 50% population increase since 1994, despite significant challenges. While overcrowded classrooms and resource shortag...
